agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order lifecycle management for a system where orders progress through placement, invoicing, and shipping. Three stakeholder groups have provided conflicting requirements about when cancellation is permitted.","bounded_context":"Order Management / Order Lifecycle","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's request to purchase goods, progressing through a lifecycle of placement, invoicing, and shipping."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"The act of voiding an order, preventing it from being fulfilled and shipped."},{"term":"Invoicing","definition":"The generation of a financial document that locks in pricing for an order. Described by Finance as irreversible."},{"term":"Shipment","definition":"The physical dispatch of goods to the customer, representing a point-of-no-return in the fulfillment process."},{"term":"Order Placement","definition":"The initial creation of an order in the system, at which point Operations states invoicing occurs immediately."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Order placed and immediately cancelled","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — PM says allowed (not yet shipped), Finance says forbidden (already invoiced at placement per Ops)."},{"scenario":"Order placed, not yet shipped, customer requests cancellation","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — same contradiction applies to the entire window between placement and shipment."},{"scenario":"Partial shipment scenario","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — if only part of an order ships, cancellation rules for the remainder are ambiguous even without the core contradiction."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"HIGH — if we follow Finance's rule literally, customers can never cancel orders, which may increase chargebacks, returns, and customer churn. If we follow the PM's rule, we must reverse invoices, which Finance explicitly says is impossible.","operational":"HIGH — Operations invoices at placement to lock pricing. Any cancellation policy that requires invoice reversal breaks this workflow and potentially destabilizes pricing guarantees.","user":"HIGH — customers expect to cancel orders before shipment. A system that forbids all cancellations post-placement will generate support escalations and erode trust.","risk":"CRITICAL — implementing either interpretation without resolving the contradiction will violate one stakeholder's stated constraint. Shipping incorrect logic here has downstream effects on accounting, refund workflows, and customer satisfaction.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Cannot cancel orders at all if Finance+Ops rules are taken literally, leading to poor experience and potential regulatory issues (consumer protection laws often mandate cancellation windows)."},{"group":"Finance Team","impact":"If PM's rule is implemented, invoices must be reversible — contradicting their stated constraint. Credit notes or void invoices may be needed."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"If invoicing is decoupled from placement to allow cancellation, their pricing-lock mechanism breaks."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will bear the brunt of whichever interpretation is chosen — either handling angry customers who cannot cancel, or handling complex invoice reversal cases."},{"group":"Product Management","impact":"Their stated requirement is undeliverable as-is given the other two constraints."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Three authoritative stakeholder groups have provided requirements that form a logical contradiction. The PM's rule (cancel before ship) is mutually exclusive with Finance's rule (no cancel after invoice) combined with Ops' rule (invoice at placement). This is not ambiguity — it is a hard contradiction. No correct implementation exists until stakeholders align. Confidence is 40 because the spec as stated is internally inconsistent.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction is real and not a matter of interpretation — the three statements are logically incompatible.","Invoicing and cancellation are the core tension points.","Shipment represents a hard physical constraint on cancellation."],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Finance would accept credit notes or void invoices as a workaround (they said 'irreversible' but may mean the original invoice persists while a compensating document is created).","Whether Operations could delay invoicing slightly (e.g., invoice at pick/pack instead of placement) while still achieving their pricing-lock goal.","Whether there are regulatory cancellation requirements that override all three stakeholder positions."],"assumptions":["All three statements are authoritative and accurately represent each team's actual constraint.","'Invoiced' and 'placed' are effectively simultaneous per Ops' statement.","There is no existing compensating mechanism (e.g., credit notes) that Finance considers distinct from 'cancelling an invoice'."]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The three requirements form a logical impossibility: (1) PM says cancel anytime before shipment, (2) Finance says cannot cancel after invoicing, (3) Ops says invoicing happens at placement. Combined: orders are invoiced the moment they are placed, so they can never be cancelled — directly contradicting the PM's requirement. No implementation can satisfy all three constraints simultaneously.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Finance Team","Operations Team","Customers","Customer Support"],"options":["A: Follow PM — allow cancellation before shipment, require Finance to accept invoice reversal via credit notes or void documents. Ops keeps invoice-at-placement.","B: Follow Finance+Ops literally — no cancellations after placement. PM must retract or redefine the cancellation feature (e.g., 'return' instead of 'cancel').","C: Decouple invoicing from placement — Ops delays invoicing to a later stage (e.g., pick/pack/ship), preserving a cancellation window. Finance constraint is honored, PM gets a cancellation window, but Ops loses immediate pricing lock.","D: Introduce an 'invoice hold' or 'pro-forma invoice' concept — a lightweight pricing lock at placement that is reversible, with the real (irreversible) invoice generated later. Satisfies all three if stakeholders accept the new terminology."],"recommendation":"Option D. Introduce a pro-forma or draft invoice at placement to lock pricing (satisfying Ops), defer the final irreversible invoice to a later fulfillment stage (satisfying Finance), and allow cancellation in the window between placement and final invoicing (satisfying PM). This requires all three teams to agree on the new lifecycle stage. Escalate to all three stakeholders for alignment before any implementation begins."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement order cancellation feature as specified","business_rule_broken":"PM's cancellation rule, Finance's invoice irreversibility rule, and Ops' invoice-at-placement rule are mutually exclusive. Any implementation will violate at least one."}]}
